{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the sensory nerve supply of the capsule of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ). Reviewing the documents, Document [1] states 'The lateral region of the capsule is innervated by the auriculotemporal nerve, also a branch of the mandibular division of cranial nerve V.' This indicates that the auriculotemporal nerve provides sensory innervation to the lateral region of the TMJ capsule.', "answer_choice": "A"}
